title: Provider's Role: Importance of Vaccine Administration and Vaccine Storage & Handling | Vaccines & Immunizations | CDC
text:
Menu 
Provider's Role: Importance of Vaccine Administration and Vaccine Storage & Handling 
July 17, 2025 
Key points 
The United States currently has the safest, most effective vaccines in its history. 
As an immunization provider, you also play a key role in helping to ensure the safety and efficacy of vaccines. 
Recommendations and guidelines 
The United States currently has the safest, most effective vaccines in its history. Federal regulations require that vaccines undergo years of testing before they can be licensed. Once in use, vaccines are monitored continually for safety and efficacy. As an immunization provider, you also play a key role in helping to ensure the safety and efficacy of vaccines through proper:
Vaccine storage and handling 
Vaccine administration 
Timing and spacing of vaccine doses 
Observation of precautions and contraindications 
Management of vaccine side effects 
Reporting of suspected side effects 
Communication about vaccine benefits and risks 
Standards for Immunization Practices for children and adolescents and adults 
Vaccine storage and administration 
To achieve the best possible results from vaccines, carefully follow the recommendations for storage, handling, and administration found in each vaccine’s package insert. Here are other steps you can take to help ensure vaccine safety:
Storage 
Carefully select and use the proper vaccine
